excerpt: The realm of spirit (ʿālam rūḥānī) precedes the realm of body (ʿālam jasmanī), just as the self precedes the body.
source_location_hint: Doctrinal exposition on inner and outer dimensions of law

excerpt: Allah alone (aḥad) stands without counterpart; all else besides Him must couple.
source_authority: Collected sermons of ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: Sermon on divine unity and creation in pairs

excerpt: Those from among the people of truth who follow the Shariah by understanding both the example and the exemplified rank among the foremost and the last.
source_location_hint: Discussion of parable and reality in faith

excerpt: All the prophets established examples for their laws, permitting one to move from the visible example to the inner exemplified truth. Noah made the ark his example. Abraham made the building. Jesus made the cross. Muhammad made the testimony of faith their example.
source_work: Jaʿfar ibn Mansur al-Yaman, Asrar al-Nutaqaʾ
source_location_hint: Exposition on prophetic examples and laws

excerpt: Each circle has a chief (rafīq). We call it the Supreme Example (al-mithāl al-aʿlā) because we cannot fathom His majesty. Although they are not Allah, they possess all His powers.
source_location_hint: Discourse on divine manifestation through ranks of beings

excerpt: The numerical value of al-naʿīm (the Bliss) equals that of sulaymānī (Sulaimani), both summing to 201.

The allegorical verses (mutashābihāt) remain ambiguous to most. Their hidden meanings reside within the veils of language. None knows their true interpretation save Allah and those firmly rooted in knowledge (al-rāsikhūn fī al-ʿilm). These are the Imams of the People of the House.
source_location_hint: Commentary on Qur'anic exegesis and Imamic authority

excerpt: He rendered knowledge obligatory on the soul because it is subtle; He rendered action obligatory on the body because it is dense. We intone in prayer: 'I bring the subtle face-to-face with the subtle, and the dense face-to-face with the dense.'
source_location_hint: Doctrine of action and knowledge in Ismaili practice

excerpt: The Commander of the Faithful, ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib, is called the 'Partial Rising One' (qāʾim juzʾī), for he enacted the duty of spiritual struggle in reality—at Jamal, Ṣiffīn, and Nahrawān.
source_authority: Collected sermons of ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib
source_location_hint: ʿAlī's exemplary implementation of divine commands

excerpt: The most blessed and noble deed in Allah's sight, the most excellent of human acts, is that which only those of specific rank can perform—the station of the law-giver (nāṭiq). In Allah's view, the formulation of the Shariah constitutes the highest and finest deed.
source_location_hint: On the rank and excellence of prophetic legislation

excerpt: Interpretation is a bridge to truths. Through interpretation, one reaches the truths which constitute the true destination. Once the bridge is crossed, it is no longer needed.
